utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive launch plans that pass all evaluation criteria. However, wi_skills is meaningfully superior in several dimensions: (1) It explicitly applies the ORB (Owned/Rented/Borrowed) framework as a named organizing principle, providing clearer strategic rationale for channel role differentiation. (2) It is substantially longer (25KB vs 18KB) with more granular weekly actions, specific budget breakdowns, and a go/no-go gate. (3) It names specific contacts (Alex Mercer) and provides pitch framing tied directly to launch timing. (4) The 'Immediate Next 5 Business Days' section is crisper and more prioritized. (5) The strategic thesis section explicitly addresses what NOT to do, which is a mark of a more disciplined plan. The wo_skills plan is surprisingly strong — it clearly read the context files carefully and produced a structured, phased plan that references borrowed channel contacts specifically — but it lacks the explicit ORB framework labeling, the go/no-go mechanics, and the same level of operational granularity. Conclusion: wi_skills produces a noticeably more strategic and actionable document, though the gap is smaller than expected given the wo_skills agent demonstrated good contextual reasoning independently.

utility_details.judge_error: Both wi_skills and wo_skills runs failed the test_output_file_min_word_count verifier check, suggesting the word count check threshold may be stricter than expected given both files are ~19KB in size. This appears to be a verifier configuration issue rather than an agent execution error. No timeout or runtime errors observed in either run.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced comprehensive, high-quality playbooks that address all seven evaluation criteria. The wi_skills run explicitly invoked the /launch-strategy skill and produced a playbook with slightly more structured framework language (owned/rented/borrowed channel taxonomy, explicit launch principles numbered list, recommended focus order). The wo_skills run produced an equally detailed playbook that also addressed all seven criteria with comparable depth and specificity. Both outputs are substantively similar in quality: both treat PH community warming as a multi-week effort, both prioritize email capture as the #1 pre-launch fix, both include all-day launch-day commitment with time-blocked schedules, both give the newsletter a specific timing and role, both provide specific listing asset requirements, both separate value-first Slack engagement from promotion, and both include concrete post-launch follow-up actions. The difference between the two is marginal — the wi_skills output has a slightly more structured framework and explicit channel taxonomy from the skill, but the wo_skills agent also read the context files thoroughly and produced strategy at a similar depth. Neither output fits the predicted 'baseline generic advice' pattern described in the test rationale. Overall quality: wi_skills is marginally better due to the structured framework and clearer prioritization order, but both outputs pass all seven judge evaluation items.
